title: Agenesis of Corpus Callosum: A Systematic Approach
objectives: Understand the pathophysiology of Agenesis of Corpus Callosum
Identify key diagnostic features of Agenesis of Corpus Callosum
Apply clinical reasoning to Agenesis of Corpus Callosum management
Recognize common pitfalls in Agenesis of Corpus Callosum

sections.title: Overview of Agenesis of Corpus Callosum
sections.content:
**Agenesis of Corpus Callosum**

Development of commissural fibers, complete vs partial agenesis, Probst bundles, associated syndromes

**Key Points:**
• Corpus callosum develops anterior to posterior (genu → body → splenium → rostrum)
• Partial agenesis affects posterior portions (splenium) since they form last
• Probst bundles: longitudinal callosal fibers that fail to cross (run parallel instead)

sections.title: Pathophysiology & Key Features
sections.content:
• Corpus callosum develops anterior to posterior (genu → body → splenium → rostrum)
• Partial agenesis affects posterior portions (splenium) since they form last

sections.title: Diagnosis & Management
sections.content:
• Probst bundles: longitudinal callosal fibers that fail to cross (run parallel instead)
• Wide range of outcomes: normal intelligence to severe disability depending on associated anomalies
• Associated syndromes: Aicardi (girls, infantile spasms, chorioretinal lacunae), Dandy-Walker, trisomies

sections.title: Clinical Pearls
sections.content:
**High-Yield Points for Agenesis of Corpus Callosum:**

• Probst bundles: longitudinal callosal fibers that fail to cross (run parallel instead)
• Wide range of outcomes: normal intelligence to severe disability depending on associated anomalies
• Associated syndromes: Aicardi (girls, infantile spasms, chorioretinal lacunae), Dandy-Walker, trisomies
